sutta: once when the exalted 1 dwelt near savarti in anata pindika's palm and jeter grove a great sacrifice was being prepared by for brahmin ogata sarira 500 bulls 500 steers and as many haifa's goats and rams were brought to the post for sacrifice now brahman ugatta sarira went and visited the exalted 1 greeted him exchanged the usual polite talk and sat down at 1 side so seated he said master gotama i've heard this the laying of the fire and the setting up of the pillar are very fruitful very advantageous and the buddha said by me too brahmin has that been heard and the brahmana brahmin spoke a second time in light manner to the exalted 1 and the exalted 1 replied as before and the third time the brahman said master gautama i've heard this the laying of the fire and the setting up of the pillar are very fruitful very advantageous and buddha said by me too brahmin has that been heard for the third time then the brahmin said this unites us master gutama even master gotama and ourselves yes all in all now when he had spoken the vulnerable ananda said this for brahmin ought not to be questioned thus i've heard that the fire and pillar of sacrifice are very advantageous it is thus the ought to be questioned i am indeed anxious lord to lay the fire to set up the pillar lord let the exalted 1 counsel me let the blessed 1 instruct me so that it may be for my happiness my welfare for many a day i just stopped here to commend her you see this brahmin he didn't know how to ask properly and ask humbly he actually he wanted the buddha's advice to confirm that this sacrifice that he was going to perform would be very meritorious but instead of asking directly this question he said then he heard that the laying of the fire and setting up with the pillar very fruitful very advantageous and the brahmin and the buddha said i've also heard that something to that effect and the second time he asked he said the same thing and the second time the buddha replied as before and the third time so the brahmananda thought that this brahman didn't know how to ask this question and he'd advise him to ask this question properly this applies to many people sometimes when you want to seek the advice of a man you must ask also directly what it is you want to know and otherwise it might not be uncertain or you might be misunderstood then the suta continues then brahman ugatta sarira said to the exalted 1 i am indeed anxious master gotama to lay the fire to set up the pillar let master gotama council and instruct me for my happiness and welfare for many a day [Music] and the buddha said brahmin even before the sacrifice a man who lays the fire who sets up the pillar sets up 3 swords evil ill in yield ill in fruit what 3 the mind sought the word sword the body saw even before the sacrifice brahmin a man laying the fire setting up the pillar causes to arise such thoughts as let there be slain for the sacrifice so many bulls steers hivers goats ramps thinking to make merit he makes the merit thinking to do good he does evil thinking he seeks the way of happy going he sees the way of ill going brahmin even before the sacrifice a man laying the fire setting up the pillar sets up firstly this mind sword which is evil ill in the eel healing fruit again brahman even before the sacrifice he speaks such words as let there be slain so many bulls steers heifers goats ramps thinking to make merit he makes thee merit even before the sacrifice he laying the fire setting up the pillar sets up secondly this speech sword forever brahman even before the sacrifice he himself first sets on foot in the business saying let them slay bulls steers high fists goats and ramps thinking to make merit he makes demerit thinking to do good he does evil thinking he seeks the way of happy going he seeks the way of evil going he sets up thirdly this body sword even before the sacrifice brahman a man laying the fire setting up the pillar sets up these 3 evil swords ill and yield ill in fruit stop here again to commander here the buddha is saying that when this person is making the preparation for the sacrifice of these animals to be killed to be offered up to heaven he does evil karma by these 3 ways in thought in the inter means in the mind and the mind by speech and by body firstly in the mind when he has intention not to kill so many bulls tears heifers etc then he thinking to make merit now he makes the merit now thinking to do good he does evil and similarly when he orders the people to kill the animals then he makes the verbal evil kama and then the third 1 when the when he starts the killing usually it seems that these brahmins when the sacrifice is started the brahmin himself will be the first to kill the animal and then he lets the workmen do the rest so in this third way he sets up this evil deep so these are the 3 ways to the mind through the speech and through the action and this is quite relevant to us today because just recently we had this uh the eighth day of the new year the chinese new year and in penang i was there recently it seems that many many animals were slaughtered for offering up to sakadevarajya and so these people in the same boat as this brahmin actually intending to make marina they are making demerit intending to get a good result of their deeds they are getting an evil result of their deeds so that they would have to pay for this evil deed in future so that's why by studying the sutas we learn to avoid the evil ways of conduct and and walk the skillful way these 3 fires ought to be forsaken avoided what 3 the fires of passion hatred and delusion raga dosa and moha and why ought the fire of passion to be forsaken avoided with mind in passion mastered obsessed by passion he takes a cause ill by body ill by speech ill by mind so doing on the breaking up of the body after death he arises in the untoward way the ill way the abyss hell therefore brahman this fire of passion is to be forsaken shunned avoided and why ought the fire of hatred to be forsaken avoided with mind perverted mastered obsessed by hatred he takes a course ill by body speech and mind and so doing he arises in hell therefore brahman this fire of hatred is to be forsaken shan avoided and why ought the fire of delusion to be forsaken shun avoided with mind deluded mastered obsessed by delusion he takes a course ill by body speech and mind and so doing he arises in hell therefore brahman this fire of delusion is to be forsaken avoided verily brahman these 3 fires ought to be forsaken avoided so i stopped here to comment again the buddha is saying are these 3 poisons the 3 defilements of passion hatred and delusion if they obsess us and if they control us and then we take a wrong cause and we perform wrong karma evil karma through the body speech and mind and because of that now we take rebirth in the woeful plains of existence then the buddha said brahman these 3 fires when esteemed revered venerated respected must bring the best happiness what 3 the fires of the venerable the householder the gift worthy and what is the fire of the venerable consider brahman the man who honors his mother and his father this is called the fire of the venerable and why from it this veneration has become therefore brahmin this fire of the variable when esteemed revered venerated respected must bring the best happiness and what is the fire of the householder consider brahman the man who honors his sons womenfolk slaves messengers workmen this is called the fire of the householder therefore brahmanan this fire of the householder when esteemed must bring the best happiness and what is the fire of the gift worthy consider brahmin those recluses and brahmins who abstain from pride and indolence who bear things patiently and neatly each taming the self each calming the self each cooling the self this is called the fire of the gift worthy therefore brahman this fire of the gift-worthy when esteemed revered venerated respected must bring the best happiness verily brahman these 3 fires when esteemed revered venerated respected must bring the best happiness now this wood fire brahmin has to be kindle from time to time has to be tended from time to time has to be quenched from time to time has to be laid from time to time and when he had thus spoken brahman ugata sarira said to the exalted 1 it's amazing master gautama it's wonderful master gotama let master gotama accept me as a lay disciple from this day forth as long as i live as 1 gone to refuge i set free these 500 bulls master gotama i give them life i set free these tears heifers goats and rams i give them life let them eat the green grass let them drink the cold water let the fresh breeze blow upon them that's the end of the suta
commentary: so in the end the buddha is saying that the instead of the 3 poisons the 3 fires of passion hatred and delusion a person should venerate 3 other types of fires not the fire of the venerable that means honoring uh taking care support of mother and father and then the second 1 the fire of the householder is to take care of the family and the workmen the workers etc the slaves etc and then the third the fire of the gift worthy is to make offerings to recluses and brahmanas who cultivate the holy life well and these 3 fires has to be kindled from time to time has to be tended from time to time has to be taken care of well taken care of and then the end the brahmin took refuge in the buddha this is a typical case of [Music] we see in the suta where these brahmins some of them are being traditional brahmins but after they talk to the buddha they became converted by the buddha and became buddhist instead of brahmins and in this utah we find that these brahmins they are in the habit of offering sacrifice to heaven and their way of offering sacrifice is after killing these animals they put them to the fire and burn them and they worship the fire and uh because of that now they even up to today they still are in brahmins are in the habitat of keeping a flame burning 24 hours a day and 365 or 366 days a year and even up to today if you go to the banks of the ganges in india you find some temples where they have this fire burning continuously every day and if a flood comes in they will quickly take the flame onto higher grounds so that the flame is not put out the nexuta is quite a good important suta a
